title: ferrotorch-core: EinsumBackwardSingle invalid backward equation for projection cases ("ij->i") — surfaced by phase 2.6 (#768)
description:
EinsumBackwardSingle::backward in einsum.rs constructs the backward equation as 'reverse_eq = format!("{rhs}->{lhs}")' for the non-trace projection branch.

For a forward equation like 'ij->i' (axis sum), this produces the backward 'i->ij' — which has output index 'j' that does not appear on the input side. The subsequent einsum call rejects it with:

  einsum: output index 'j' does not appear in any input subscripts

Repro:
  let a: Tensor<f32> = leaf([2, 3], requires_grad=true);
  let r = einsum_differentiable("ij->i", &[&a]);
  let loss = sum(&r);
  loss.backward();   // panics with InvalidArgument

Same shape applies to any single-input einsum where the LHS is a strict superset of the RHS (axis-sum / axis-projection). Pure permutations like 'ij->ji' and trace 'ii->' have separate code paths that work.

The correct backward for projection cases ('ij->i') is to broadcast grad_output along the dropped axes — analogous to SumDimBackward — not to swap LHS/RHS.

Surfaced by phase 2.6 conformance test cpu_einsum_differentiable on the 'axis_sum' fixture; reproduces on GPU as well via gpu::gpu_einsum_differentiable.
